caseStudies.title: ML-Powered Search Ranking
caseStudies.description: Replacing heuristic search ranking with a learned model.
caseStudies.overview: An e-commerce platform's product search relied on hand-tuned heuristic ranking rules that had accumulated over a decade. The rules were brittle, hard to maintain, and increasingly misaligned with user behavior as the product catalog expanded to over 5 million SKUs.
caseStudies.problem: Search relevance had stagnated, with 23% of search sessions ending without a click — a strong signal that results were failing users. The heuristic rules required weeks of expert tuning for each new product category, making expansion costly.
caseStudies.solution: A learning-to-rank model was trained on historical click and purchase signals, replacing the heuristic system. A/B testing infrastructure was built to evaluate ranking experiments safely. The model was retrained weekly on fresh behavioral data and served with sub-10ms latency via an optimized inference layer.

results.summary: The ML ranking system significantly outperforms the legacy heuristics across all product categories. The self-improving nature of the model means relevance continues to increase as more behavioral data accumulates.

caseStudies.title: Data Warehouse Consolidation
caseStudies.description: Unifying 14 siloed analytics databases into a single platform.
caseStudies.overview: A media conglomerate operated 14 separate analytics databases accumulated through acquisitions, each with different schemas, access controls, and tooling. Cross-brand reporting required manual data reconciliation taking analysts days per report.
caseStudies.problem: Leadership had no unified view of business performance across brands. Analysts spent 60% of their time on data wrangling rather than analysis. Inconsistent metric definitions across brands made comparisons unreliable.
caseStudies.solution: A centralised cloud data warehouse was established as the single source of truth. Data from all 14 source systems was ingested via standardised connectors, with a semantic layer enforcing consistent metric definitions. Self-service BI tooling was rolled out with role-based access control to ensure data governance.

results.summary: Executives now access consistent, real-time performance dashboards across all brands. Analysts have shifted from data preparation to delivering strategic insights that drive business decisions.

caseStudies.title: Ads Fraud Detection System
caseStudies.description: Real-time invalid traffic detection protecting ad spend.
caseStudies.overview: An ad-tech platform was experiencing significant invalid traffic affecting campaign performance metrics and eroding advertiser trust. The rule-based detection system was reactive, catching fraud patterns only after significant spend had been wasted.
caseStudies.problem: Sophisticated bot networks mimicked human browsing behavior, evading rule-based filters. Advertisers were reporting discrepancies between platform metrics and third-party measurement tools, leading to disputes and churn.
caseStudies.solution: A multi-layered anomaly detection system was built, combining device fingerprinting, behavioral biometrics, and graph-based network analysis to identify coordinated invalid traffic patterns. Real-time scoring allowed suspicious traffic to be filtered before impressions were served, with human review queues for edge cases.

results.summary: Advertiser confidence in platform metrics was restored, reversing a trend of measurement-related churn. The system's continuous learning loop means it adapts to new fraud patterns as they emerge.
